judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are substantively equivalent, correctly identifying the same root cause, commit, flawed reproduction method, and flawed proposed fix, with report 2 offering slightly more detail on the dual-source-of-truth nature of the defect and git history verification.

report_1.evidence: config/scoring_config.yaml sets expected_label_field: label, but every record in fixtures/eval_set.jsonl now stores its gold label under the key gold_label (not label). dict.get("label") on those records therefore always returns None... The fix is a one-line config change: in ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml, change expected_label_field: label to expected_label_field: gold_label

report_2.evidence: config/scoring_config.yaml still says expected_label_field: label, while every record in fixtures/eval_set.jsonl now uses the key "gold_label"... The file to change is ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml: update expected_label_field: label to expected_label_field: gold_label

report_1.evidence: The regression began on 2026-06-17, exactly matching commit 53551e8 — chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)... pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 through 2026-06-16 and exactly 0.00 starting 2026-06-17, the same date as the commit

report_2.evidence: pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv shows accuracy going from 0.91 on 2026-06-16 straight to 0.00 on 2026-06-17 with no partial/transitional values. This lines up exactly with commit 53551e8 — "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"

report_1.evidence: it re-ran individual tickets through pipeline/score_one.py, but that script (read above) never loads the config and never compares its prediction against any gold label at all — it just prints the predicted category for a human to eyeball... it was testing a code path that structurally cannot exhibit this bug.

report_2.evidence: pipeline/score_one.py (used for the manual spot-check re-runs) only calls predict_category(normalize_simple(text)) — it never reads scoring_config.yaml, never reads a gold label field, and never performs the prediction == gold comparison that's actually broken. It is structurally incapable of surfacing this bug

report_1.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it targets the wrong mechanism and will not fix anything... 'Reverting the model' would touch a field nothing consumes, leave expected_label_field: label unchanged, and the nightly accuracy would remain exactly 0.0 the next night.

report_2.evidence: This plan should not be acted on — it targets the wrong layer of the system and would not fix anything... Swapping model_id or 'reverting the model' would leave expected_label_field: label unchanged, fixtures/eval_set.jsonl would still use gold_label, and the batch score would still be 0.0.
